MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar May-04-2021 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Feb-17-2021 Continued To Jul-26-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Feb-23-2021 Continued To May-14-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Oct-23-2020 Continued To Apr-05-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-23-2020 Set For Trial On Jan-04-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-18-2020 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT AEVOE CORP's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled. Defendant fails to show that res judicata or claim preclusion bars the instant action. The complaint is not uncertain.

Judicially noticeable documents show that a similar discrimination action was brought against defendant in New York federal district court (Dennis). That putative class action sought relief on behalf of Mr. Dennis as well as "all others similarly situated." (Defendant's Request for Judicial Notice, Ex.1.) The Dennis action settled and the court dismissed it with prejudice. Defendant does not supply the settlement agreement, and there is no indication in the Stipulation and Voluntary Dismissal with Prejudice that the putative class claims were also settled. (Id. at Ex. 2.) In fact, that document provides that "Derrick U Dennis ('Plaintiff')"-not the plural plaintiffs-settled the matter. Moreover, a review of the federal docket does not show that the federal court ever approved class treatment of the Dennis action or that notice was provided to the class members. In such circumstances, the court cannot conclude that the Dennis action has a preclusive effect on the instant action. (See Shine v. Williams-Sonoma, Inc. (2018) 23 Cal.App.5th 1070, 1076 ["This preclusive effect applies where, as here, the previous action was dismissed with prejudice based on a court-approved class action settlement agreement."]; Louie v. BFS Retail & Commercial Operations, LLC (2009) 178 Cal.App.4th 1544, 1555 ["Under federal and California law, a judgment in a class action is binding on class members in any subsequent litigation, though the ability to bind absent class members depends on compliance with due process regarding notice and adequate representation."]; Richards v. Jefferson County (1996) 517 U.S. 793, 798-801 [Alabama state court violated due process by concluding challengers to county tax were bound, under state law as to res judicata, by prior adjudication to which challengers had not been parties].)

The court rejects defendant's mootness argument as that raises factual issues outside the four corners of the complaint. The court further rejects defendant's argument that plaintiff does not plead sufficient facts regarding intentional discrimination as that argument was raised for the first time in the reply brief. (See American Indian Model Schools v. Oakland Unified School Dist. (2014) 227 Cal.App.4th 258, 275 ["We will not ordinarily consider issues raised for the first time in a reply brief."].)

The Court signed the proposed order submitted by email.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.
